subject: english
learning_objectives: Identify and define key terms related to air transportation
Identify and define key terms related to sea (maritime) transportation
Identify and define key terms related to rail transportation

sections.heading: Air Transportation
sections.content:
taxi – the slow movement of a plane along the ground before take-off
runway – a wide path from which aircraft take off and land
tarmac – the surface of a road or airport runway (e.g. 'The plane was waiting on the tarmac.')
air hostess – a young woman who looks after passengers in an aircraft
flight – a journey in a plane
airborne – in the air; describing a plane that is flying
radar – a method of determining the direction and distance of aircraft using radio waves
touch down – the moment a plane lands
hangar – the large building where aircraft are housed
control tower – the building from which air traffic controllers direct plane traffic
cockpit – the compartment in which the pilot of an aeroplane sits

sections.heading: Sea (Maritime) Transportation
sections.content:
mast – a long pole set upright on a ship to support the sail or flag
international waters – waters beyond a country's territorial limits (always 'waters', not 'water')
berth – a place in port where a ship can be moored
boatyard – a place where boats are built or repaired
harbour – a place of shelter for ships
quayside – the side or edge of a quay
ocean liner – a large ship used to carry passengers and cargo across the ocean
cruise ship – a ship used for recreational voyages
cabin – a private room in a ship
yacht – a boat or small ship, usually with sails, used for racing or cruising
dredging – the clearing or deepening of a river or body of water
dock – a platform built on the shore (wharf)
coast – land along the sea
crew – the group of people who operate a ship
captain – the commander of a ship
offshore – in or on the sea, not far from the coast (opposite: onshore)
anchor – a heavy piece of metal that digs into the sea-bed to hold a ship in place

sections.heading: Rail Transportation
sections.content:
coach – a passenger railway carriage
freight – goods or cargo transported by rail
locomotive – a self-powered engine used to pull railway trains
railway yard – a place where trains are parked or maintained
commuter trains – trains carrying passengers between large cities and suburbs
freight service – a service transporting goods from one place to another
rail tracks – the tracks on which a train moves
